channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Like Biden, Harris puts focus on Trump as Election Day draws near
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — With the end of her abbreviated presidential campaign in sight, Kamala Harris is trying to put the focus squarely on Donald Trump and his threat to democratic institutions, echoing the strategy used by Joe Biden before he ended his reelection bid.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ship owner in Baltimore bridge collapse agrees to pay $102 million for cleanup
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The owner and manager of the cargo ship that caused the deadly Baltimore bridge collapse have agreed to pay more than $102 million in cleanup costs to settle a lawsuit brought by the Justice Department, officials said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Major Michigan city decides against verifying mail ballots early, potentially slowing results
item.description: A decision by the elections clerk in Michigan’s third-largest city is raising concerns of a slowdown in reporting election night results in a county that is being targeted by both presidential campaigns and includes a competitive congressional race.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Volmert, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Democrats give Harris rocky reviews on CNN town hall
item.description: Vice President Harris’s CNN town hall performance received tough reviews from fellow Democrats who criticized her for missing the moment to sell her candidacy as polls have started to show a shift toward former President Trump in recent days.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Harris dodges border wall question, blames Trump despite record numbers on her watch
item.description: Kamala Harris continued to criticize former President Trump Thursday after being asked about the continuation of a wall along the southern border.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: President Biden to apologize for 150-year Indian boarding school policy
item.description: President Joe Biden is planning to formally apologize for the nation's Indian boarding school system, which included at least 16 schools in Minnesota. This is the first time a president has formally apologized on the matter.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Beyonce to join Kamala Harris at red state campaign rally: report
item.description: Singer Beyoncé will join Vice President Kamala Harris for a rally in Houston on Friday, according to sources who spoke to The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Beyoncé expected to join Harris at Houston rally: Reports
item.description: Beyoncé is poised to get in "Formation" behind Vice President Kamala Harris' White House bid. The music superstar will reportedly be joining the vice president at a Friday campaign rally in Texas.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, WGN 9

item.title: Colleges left helpless as students rule out schools due to state politics
item.description: Colleges and universities find themselves increasingly trapped with the politics of the state where they reside.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Calling out of work for your pet? City Council introduces pet sick day bill
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Sick days may not just be for humans living in New York City in the future. City Councilmembers Shaun Abreu and Tiffany Cabán introduced a bill on Wednesday to let workers take sick days if their pets are ill.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Quarter of young Black men say they will vote for Trump: Survey
item.description: Despite one-fourth of young Black men saying they would cast their ballot for Trump, they repeatedly identify Harris as the strongest candidate on the issues.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, NewsNation

item.title: Two city council members vie to succeed St. Cloud’s departing mayor
item.description: The mayoral race is highlighting some of St. Cloud’s most pressing challenges, including a housing crunch, a struggle to revive its downtown and a perception that the city is less safe. Both candidates talk about addressing those problems, but they differ — sometimes subtly — on how to do so.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: Four memorable moments from Kamala Harris’ CNN town hall
item.description: Harris left no doubt that she believes Trump is a fascist and she widened her break with President Joe Biden.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: With 2 women running, the New Hampshire governor's race is both close and personal
item.description: CONCORD, N.H. (AP) — One of the nation's most competitive gubernatorial races has also become intensely personal. None of the nation’s 12 female governors are up for reelection, but five women are running as major party gubernatorial nominees in four states.
item.dc:creator: HOLLY RAMER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Nevada ready for secure, 'solid election,' secretary of state says
item.description: Nevada Secretary of State Cisco Aguilar believes the state is well poised to have a safe and secure election as hundreds of thousands of Nevadans have already voted.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: With dueling rallies this week, Georgia is on the mind for Trump and Harris
item.description: Both candidates are in the crucial swing state this week. Trump had two events on Wednesday, while Harris, ahead of her own visit, answered "Yes" at a CNN town hall when asked if Trump is a fascist.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler, MPR News

item.title: Ronna McDaniel: How Republicans can win Gen Z voters
item.description: In an election that will be won on the margins, the failure of Republicans to engage these new young voters would amount to a missed opportunity.
item.dc:creator: Ronna McDaniel, opinion contributor, NewsNation

item.title: Axelrod: Harris in a 'terrible place' on Middle East conflict
item.description: Veteran Democratic strategist David Axelrod says Vice President Harris is caught in a “terrible place” on the growing conflict in the Middle East, which threatens to turn off Lebanese and Palestinian American voters in Michigan, a crucial battleground state.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Harris to make her closing argument at the Ellipse 1 week ahead of election
item.description: Vice President Harris will give an address at the Ellipse in Washington, D.C., on Tuesday, one week before Election Day.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Democrats warn of threat to democracy in closing pitch to voters
item.description: Democrats are zeroing in on the future of democracy and former President Trump’s response to the Jan. 6 attack on the Capitol as part of their closing pitch to voters two weeks out from Election Day.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, NewsNation

item.title: Senate battle goes from bad to worse for Democrats
item.description: Senate Democrats are facing an increasingly tough road to keeping their majority in the upper chamber as races in crucial swing states tighten with just two weeks until Election Day.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: In Grand Rapids, voters keep politics mostly quiet to keep civility
item.description: Voters in Grand Rapids are concerned about the economy and other issues central to this divisive election. But in a small town where people rub shoulders with those on the other side of the political spectrum, they’re more interested in maintaining civility.
item.dc:creator: Dan Kraker, MPR News

item.title: Trump makes more debunked claims about FEMA as he visits storm damaged North Carolina
item.description: Trump was asked whether it was helpful to criticize hurricane relief workers after the Federal Emergency Management Agency recently paused work in the area because of reports they could be targeted by militia.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: New poll suggests abortion, marijuana amendments may have enough support
item.description: A new poll from the University of North Florida suggests that both Florida's abortion and recreational marijuana amendments have enough support to pass.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court turns away challenge to consumer safety agency’s structure
item.description: The Supreme Court announced Monday it won’t take up a case that could have delivered another blow to federal agencies. Two conservative groups sought to challenge the structure of the Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC), which oversees making sure the country’s consumer products are safe.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Some Tampa Bay counties require vote-by-mail postage
item.description: Early voting is underway this week, and supervisors of elections also expect an increase in the return of vote-by-mail ballots.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Facing a budget deficit challenge, Johnson aims to safeguard youth and workforce
item.description: Enticing grocery store chains to open in underserved communities is another priority for the Johnson administration.
item.dc:creator: Julian Crews, WGN 9

item.title: Trump expresses frustration about calls to deploy Haley
item.description: “But let me just tell you: Nikki Haley and I fought, and I beat her by 50, 60, 90 points. I beat her in her own state. I beat Nikki badly.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: US Senate candidates Brown, Rosen spar in Las Vegas debate
item.description: The showdown was the only face-to-face debate between Brown - the retired U.S. Army Captain - and Rosen in advance of the election on November 5.
item.dc:creator: Kyle J. Paine, KLAS, CBS News
